org_kg_contexts.name: Industry
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text:
Work with industry and the public to better identify use cases and address pain points using AI.
Most of the compute supply is expected to go toward industry use.
Singapore should continue to lean forward to support industry efforts to employ data for AI.
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
Industry enterprises may apply to the 100 Experiments programme.
Work with industry and the public to better identify use cases and address pain points using AI. This can be done through well-structured ideathons, hackathons, and incubator programmes.
Several companies, from a range of sectors, have participated in the 100E programme.
AISG helps pair companies with public researchers to develop AI solutions that address the former's problem statement, and provides co-funding of up to S$330,000 per project.
100 Experiments (100E) is AISG's flagship programme, aimed at solving AI problem statements proposed by industry.
100 Experiments (100E) is AISG's flagship programme, aimed at solving AI problem statements proposed by industry where no commercial off-the-shelf AI solutions exist.
Through Company-Led Training Programmes and Career Conversion Programmes, amongst others, TeSA has partnered industry to train individuals in AI and Data Analytics.
The AIAP is currently designed as a 9-month, full-time, deep-skilling company-led training programme, that helps trainees acquire AI capabilities by working alongside industry teams on real business problems.
TeSA has partnered industry to train individuals in AI and Data Analytics.
Through programmes like SMEs Go Digital and CTO-as-a-Service (CTOaaS), the Government has helped businesses gain awareness of suitable AI-enabled solutions.
A trusted environment where AI-enabled innovations and systems are robust and safe, so that our people can engage with AI with confidence.

org_kg_contexts.name: Enterprise
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text:
Enterprises are end-users and demand drivers for AI solutions.
Recent advances, especially in Generative AI, have opened doors to new possibilities for enterprises to adopt AI.
We must invest in our people, enterprises, and digital infrastructure.
This requires investments in our people, enterprises, and digital infrastructure, so that we can create new jobs and participate in industries of the future.
We shall uplift and empower our people and businesses, equipping them with the capabilities and resources to thrive in an AI-enabled future.
A confident base of enterprises and workers can use AI tools.
A confident base of enterprises and workers can use AI solutions.
Industry, Government, and Research drive AI activity, from basic science to product R&D and adoption.
Businesses were given free access to Google Cloud's AI toolsets for up to three months.
Many companies have already embarked on AI-enabled innovation projects, which are often focused on the Application layer.
We will work with Industry Champions to identify sector use cases and crowd in a broader base of researchers and companies in the sector.
Research breakthroughs can translate into commercial products, which can be used by our companies.
Their research breakthroughs can also translate into commercial products, which can be used by our companies and across government agencies to realise better outcomes.
100 Experiments (100E) is AISG's flagship programme, aimed at solving AI problem statements proposed by industry where no commercial off-the-shelf AI solutions exist.
Several companies, from a range of sectors, have participated in the 100E programme.
The project also drew in industry partners from both countries.
AI has the potential to help enterprises increase their productivity and stay relevant, provided they have the capabilities to apply it well.
Empower enterprises and workers to adopt AI and stay ahead of the curve.
Singapore will intensify the promotion of AI adoption across all enterprises.
We will promote baseline digital adoption for our enterprises and enhance the enterprise digitalisation toolkit, to support more sophisticated AI adoption.
For more digitally mature enterprises, we will provide tailored support for AI-enabled business transformation.
The vibrancy of the community in turn attracts the participation of even more talented individuals, companies, and capital.
The data concierge can explore facilitating handshakes between private industry entities as a trusted intermediary.
The Government will also elevate the security and resilience baseline for all system owners using AI.
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
AI Trailblazers provides free access to Google Cloud AI toolsets for up to three months.
The AI Trailblazers programme provides free access to Google Cloud AI toolsets for up to three months to generate 100 Generative AI use cases in 100 days.
More of our technology workforce should work to scale novel AI solutions, that form part of the toolkit which a confident base of enterprises and workers can use.
Foundational AI, where scientific advancements are still needed to improve AI’s abilities. For a start, we will double down on three specific themes – Reasoning AI, Resource-Efficient AI, and Responsible AI – with a view to making AI less costly, more widely-used, and most importantly, trusted.
For a start, we will double down on three specific themes – Reasoning AI, Resource-Efficient AI, and Responsible AI – with a view to making AI less costly, more widely-used, and most importantly, trusted.
Their research breakthroughs can also translate into commercial products, which can be used by our companies and across government agencies to realise better outcomes.
Workers need to utilise AI tools, so that they can increase their productivity, avoid untimely disruptions to their jobs, and help enterprises optimise their operations and improve their competitiveness.
We have made available tools that enterprises can use to evaluate their readiness to adopt AI. For example, self-assessment tools like AISG's AI Readiness Index (AIRI) are useful in helping companies identify and map out the capabilities and infrastructure they need for using AI.
This includes leveraging IMDA and EnterpriseSG’s Digital Leaders Programme (DLP), which helps companies build in-house digital capabilities, as well as the scoping of impactful AI projects in partnership with AI solution providers.
the vibrancy of the community in turn attracts the participation of even more talented individuals, companies, and capital.
Singapore-based company that participates in the Digital Leaders Programme to build in-house digital capabilities and scope AI projects.
AI Singapore evaluates project outcomes and impact.
For private sector datasets, the Government can consider stepping in for selected high-value use cases where we can play a catalytic role.

org_kg_contexts.name: Singapore Government
org_kg_contexts.role_scope: Lead AI adoption in Smart Nation priorities and issue policy guidelines
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text:
Singapore believes in the transformative potential of AI. Our second National AI Strategy represents Singapore's commitment to realise the benefits of AI.
The report sets out government plans to build a thriving AI ecosystem, develop workforce, provide infrastructure, and foster a trusted environment.
In Singapore, where the vast majority of our people already frequently interact with digital technologies to transact with the Government and businesses.
Singapore believes that AI can be a potent force for good, to uplift human potential.
The recent breakthroughs in AI demand a renewed focus by Singapore to refine our national strategies for AI.
Singapore's second National AI Strategy (NAIS 2.0) will drive the next bound of our Smart Nation journey.
NAIS 2.0 starts with the conviction that we must do our utmost to harness AI for the Public Good, for Singapore and the World.
Singapore intends to provide more platforms which can bring our AI community together.
Singapore must ensure reliable, localised access to high-performance compute, so that industry, academia, and the Government have the means to innovate and build here.
Singapore is determined to play our part, and we will continue to participate constructively at international fora on AI.
Singapore is committed to being a serious and reliable international partner on AI.
Singapore demonstrates commitment to developing and deploying AI well, and will leverage these gains to be a global pace-setter.
We will actively profile Singapore's approaches to AI through public engagements and conferences such as Asia Tech x Singapore and SCAI.
Singapore’s NAIS 2.0 is therefore designed to unfold as a national movement on the global stage.
Published 4 December 2023 Copyright © 2023 Government of the Republic of Singapore
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
This report spells out how we will achieve our vision.
It sets out our plans to build a thriving AI ecosystem
Singapore believes that AI can be a potent force for good, to uplift human potential.
This requires us to steer AI for the Public Good.
AI is strategically important. We must invest in it to unlock the next frontier of economic growth.
Address risks from the potential abuse and mismanagement of AI. These include the misuse of AI to amplify harms (e.g. scams, cyberattacks, and mis/disinformation) across society, especially toward vulnerable groups.
We will take a systems approach, bringing together stakeholders within and outside Singapore to add to our resources, capabilities, and infrastructure, accelerate the exchange of ideas, and administer AI-enabled solutions at scale.
Singapore must contribute to AI breakthroughs and products that the world values.
We will continually review these Actions to respond to fast-moving developments in AI, across domains.
We will undertake across these systems and enablers, to support our ambitions over the next 3-5 years.
We will develop and roll out targeted courses to impart AI skills, for different public sector workforce segments (e.g. senior management, policy, and operational roles).
This could take the form of joint grant calls or PhD training programmes with other countries, or involvement in international conferences.
Through programmes like SMEs Go Digital and CTO-as-a-Service (CTOaaS), the Government has helped businesses gain awareness of suitable AI-enabled solutions.
we should invest in Privacy-Enhancing Technologies (PETs), and other novel approaches that can address barriers around data protection and sharing, to encourage more data flows.
The Government provides central funding to support AI use cases in the public sector.
Singapore is determined to play our part, and we will continue to participate constructively at international fora on AI.

org_kg_contexts.name: Singapore
org_kg_contexts.role_scope: Lead AI adoption in Smart Nation priorities
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text: Singapore aspires to be a pace-setter – a global leader in choice AI areas, that are economically impactful and serve the Public Good.
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
We will participate fully in AI research and deployment.
Singapore aspires to be a pace-setter – a global leader in choice AI areas, that are economically impactful and serve the Public Good.
Singapore must contribute to AI breakthroughs and products that the world values.
This strategy statement outlines 15 Actions that Singapore will undertake across these systems and enablers, to support our ambitions over the next 3-5 years.
We will continually review these Actions to respond to fast-moving developments in AI, across domains.
We will ensure that Singapore hosts the necessary infrastructure and provides a trusted environment for AI innovation.
We hope to participate in shaping the international rules of the road that are emerging around AI.
Safeguarding Singapore’s AI interests in the international arena. We hope to participate in shaping the international rules of the road that are emerging around AI.
It remains critical for Singapore to stay engaged internationally, in a multi-party, multi-stakeholder fashion, to work towards a common understanding on AI.
We will continue to participate constructively at international fora on AI.
Singapore is committed to being a serious and reliable international partner on AI.
Continue to grow Singapore’s international networks with key partner countries and leading AI companies.
Singapore will support and actively participate in substantive multilateral, multi-stakeholder, or plurilateral initiatives, that seek to achieve an inclusive, practical, and rules-based global environment for AI.
Singapore will support and actively participate in substantive multilateral, multi-stakeholder, or plurilateral initiatives.
Anchoring key bilateral relationships with selected partners from government and industry, through substantive initiatives and technical cooperation.
actively participate in substantive multilateral, multi-stakeholder, or plurilateral initiatives, that seek to achieve an inclusive, practical, and rules-based global environment for AI.

participants.professional_persona: A mid-career data scientist who transitioned from finance after completing a TeSA Career Conversion Programme. He now leads a small team at a logistics SME, deploying predictive models for route optimization.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in Python, TensorFlow, and time-series forecasting; also skilled in stakeholder management and translating business problems into AI use cases.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to become a Chief AI Officer within five years and mentor aspiring AI practitioners from non-tech backgrounds.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Kaur is a research fellow at A*STAR's Institute for Infocomm Research, focusing on AI for social good. She recently returned to Singapore after a postdoc at ETH Zurich.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expertise in reinforcement learning, agent-based modeling, and causal inference. Experienced in collaborating with government agencies on smart city projects.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to develop AI systems that optimize public transport and reduce energy consumption in Singapore. Aspires to become a principal investigator and lead a national research programme.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-d42jondl
participants.professional_persona: A senior product manager at a health-tech company that builds AI-assisted diagnostic tools. He wants to ensure the tools are robust and safe before deployment in polyclinics.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep expertise in product lifecycle management, clinical workflows, and regulatory requirements for medical AI.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: To drive the adoption of trustworthy AI in Singapore's healthcare system and eventually shape national standards for AI in medicine.

participants.professional_persona: Omar is a data science lead at a multinational bank, where he builds fraud detection models. He holds a part-time faculty appointment at Singapore Management University.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in anomaly detection, time-series forecasting, and explainable AI. Skilled at bridging the gap between research and production systems in a regulated industry.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to publish his work on adversarial robustness for financial AI and eventually transition into a full-time academic role. Enthusiastic about mentoring junior researchers through the AI Apprenticeship Programme.

participants.professional_persona: Priya is a senior researcher at an AI startup that won the 100 Experiments grant last year. She specializes in applying AI to agricultural supply chains.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Knowledge of satellite imagery analysis, crop yield prediction models, and IoT sensor data fusion. Experienced in deploying AI solutions in resource-constrained environments.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to expand her startup's impact across Southeast Asia by securing an R&D investment grant. Hopes to reduce food waste and improve farmer livelihoods through AI.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Goh is a distinguished visiting scholar from Carnegie Mellon University, currently on sabbatical at NUS. His research focuses on reasoning AI and automated theorem proving.
participants.skills_and_expertise: World-renowned in symbolic AI, knowledge graphs, and neuro-symbolic reasoning. Has a track record of mentoring PhD students who become leading researchers in industry.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to establish a joint research lab between CMU and Singapore on trustworthy AI. Aims to contribute to Singapore's goal of becoming a global leader in reasoning AI.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-j5qrrog3
participants.professional_persona: A freelance graphic designer with a side interest in AI art tools. She participates in hackathons to explore ethical uses of generative AI for preserving local dialects and heritage.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Creative design skills, proficiency in Adobe Suite, and self-taught prompt engineering and fine-tuning of diffusion models.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: To build a platform that helps community groups create culturally relevant AI-generated content while respecting IP and authenticity.

participants.professional_persona: A fresh graduate from NUS Computer Science who joined a 100 Experiments project as an AI engineer. She enjoys prototyping generative AI solutions for retail use cases.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Proficient in PyTorch, NLP, and cloud deployment (AWS); experienced in agile development and rapid prototyping.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to publish a research paper on resource-efficient AI and later start her own AI consultancy.

participants.professional_persona: Ling is a research engineer at a government-linked digital services company. She pivoted from software engineering to AI through the Company-Led Training Programme.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Proficient in MLOps, model deployment pipelines, and cloud infrastructure. Skilled at translating business requirements into technical AI solutions.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to become a lead AI architect and eventually move into a research role focused on applied AI for public services. Excited about contributing to Singapore's Smart Nation initiatives.

participants.professional_persona: Raj is a senior AI researcher at a startup that develops AI-driven drug discovery platforms. He previously worked at a biotech firm in Boston.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in graph neural networks, molecular docking simulations, and high-throughput screening data analysis. Skilled in interdisciplinary collaboration with chemists and biologists.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to accelerate the discovery of novel therapeutics for tropical diseases. Wants to collaborate with Singapore's research hospitals through the 100 Experiments programme.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-4o6xoajz
participants.professional_persona: A mid-level civil servant in the Government Technology Agency (GovTech) tasked with procuring AI solutions for public services. He is keen to pilot a chatbot for citizens seeking housing grants.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Knowledge of public procurement policies, agile project management, and vendor evaluation; completed a certificate in AI for Public Policy.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: To become a digital transformation lead in the public sector, ensuring that government AI projects deliver tangible public good and are trusted.

participants.professional_persona: A veteran software architect in his 40s who pivoted into AI/ML through company-led training. He now designs scalable inference pipelines for a fintech firm.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep expertise in MLOps, Kubernetes, and model monitoring; strong background in distributed systems and cloud-native architecture.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to drive AI adoption in the financial sector and contribute to Singapore's trusted AI guidelines.

participants.professional_persona: Mei is an independent researcher and consultant who advises SMEs on AI adoption. She previously held a tenure-track position in Japan and moved back to Singapore for family reasons.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Broad knowledge of AI applications across logistics, retail, and finance. Special talent for explaining complex AI concepts to non-technical stakeholders and crafting pilot projects.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to build a portfolio of successful AI adoption stories among local SMEs. Plans to author a practical guidebook on AI for small businesses and eventually join a government taskforce on AI adoption.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-l9bblyp8

participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in geospatial data analysis and simulation modelling, with a growing proficiency in machine learning for predictive urban planning.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to lead a cross-agency pilot that uses AI to reduce peak-hour congestion and improve public transport efficiency, setting a benchmark for 'AI for Public Good' in smart cities.

participants.professional_persona: A self-taught AI specialist from a polytechnic background who rose through TeSA's Company-Led Training. She now builds computer vision models for a manufacturing SME.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Skilled in OpenCV, YOLO, and edge deployment; adept at low-resource model optimization and working with limited data.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Hopes to lead a team that brings AI to traditional industries and eventually pursue a part-time Master's in AI.

participants.professional_persona: An experienced AI researcher from a government research lab who now works as a senior AI practitioner at an enterprise. He focuses on responsible AI and bias mitigation.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in fairness metrics, adversarial debiasing, and interpretability tools; published in top AI conferences.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to shape national AI ethics standards and eventually become a policy advisor on AI governance.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Mei Ling is a senior research scientist at a government-funded AI lab in Singapore, where she focuses on developing resource-efficient deep learning models. She previously spent six years leading a team at a multinational tech corporation, and now thrives on bridging foundational research with real-world industry applications.
participants.skills_and_expertise: She specializes in reasoning AI, model compression, and privacy-enhancing technologies, with a strong track record of deploying scalable AI solutions in production environments.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: She aims to push Singapore's leadership in trusted AI by pioneering low-cost, high-accuracy models that small and medium enterprises can adopt, and eventually to serve on national advisory committees shaping AI policy.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-9s3qtvav
participants.professional_persona: A former teacher turned edtech entrepreneur, he runs a start-up that creates personalised learning tools for students with dyslexia. He is passionate about making AI accessible for inclusive education.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Skilled in natural language processing and user-centred design, with hands-on experience deploying AI prototypes in school settings.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to scale his solution through the 100 Experiments programme, proving that AI can democratise learning for neurodiverse students across Southeast Asia.

participants.professional_persona: Ravi is an AI researcher at a fast-growing Singapore-based startup that develops Generative AI tools for the logistics sector. After a decade as a software engineer, he pivoted into AI research and now leads the company's participation in the 100 Experiments programme.
participants.skills_and_expertise: His expertise covers applied natural language processing, transfer learning, and productizing research prototypes, with a knack for identifying high-impact use cases from industry pain points.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: He wants to demonstrate that locally developed AI can compete globally, and hopes to scale his startup's solutions across Southeast Asia while mentoring the next generation of AI practitioners.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to implement a predictive maintenance and routing solution that saves his company 20% in operational costs, demonstrating AI’s value for traditional industries.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to join a public-sector AI team that builds an early-warning system for falls in nursing homes, contributing to ‘AI for Public Good’ in healthcare.

participants.skills_and_expertise: Strong in Spark, SQL, and feature engineering; knowledgeable about healthcare data privacy regulations and HL7 FHIR.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to specialize in AI for healthcare and help Singapore become a hub for medical AI innovation.

participants.professional_persona: A female AI product manager who previously worked in business development. She bridges domain experts and technical teams in a Smart Nation project.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in AI product lifecycle, requirements gathering, and user testing; familiar with no-code AI platforms and responsible AI checklists.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aspires to launch a public-sector AI solution that impacts millions of Singaporeans and later consult on AI strategy.

participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep understanding of adversarial machine learning, model poisoning defenses, and secure enclaves; also skilled in penetration testing.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to become a lead AI security architect and contribute to Singapore's trusted AI environment.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-ssgtlkyx
participants.professional_persona: A senior banker in a local bank, he is leading the digital transformation of retail lending. He sees responsible AI as crucial for fair credit scoring and fraud detection.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in risk management and regulatory compliance, with recent training in explainable AI (XAI) through executive courses.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to launch an AI-driven credit model that expands access to loans for underserved small businesses, while maintaining transparency and trust.

participants.professional_persona: A mid-career professional who re-skilled via TeSA after 10 years in marketing. She now applies natural language processing to customer analytics for a retail enterprise.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Skilled in sentiment analysis, chatbot development, and A/B testing; able to communicate AI value to non-technical stakeholders.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to lead a team that uses AI to personalize customer experiences and eventually teach AI literacy to marketers.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Hopes to create a repository of reusable AI components for common SME pain points (e.g., inventory forecasting), fostering a collaborative community of practice.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Hopes to advance warehouse automation in Singapore and eventually pursue a PhD in robotics.

participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in deep learning, natural language processing, and MLOps. Experienced in deploying models with strict regulatory compliance in the financial sector.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to become a Chief AI Officer in a local enterprise and contribute to shaping Singapore's AI governance standards for the financial industry.

participants.professional_persona: Mei Ling is a principal AI engineer at a government-linked research institute, where she leads projects on responsible AI and fairness auditing. participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to co-design a pilot that uses AI to predict patient no-shows, reducing healthcare waste, while setting a strong ethical framework for AI use in public services.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-203ct5ev
participants.professional_persona: A social science researcher at a local university, she studies algorithmic fairness and bias. She wants her work to inform government guidelines on inclusive AI.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Mixed-methods research (surveys, interviews, quantitative fairness metrics) and experience publishing on AI ethics in Southeast Asian contexts.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to collaborate with AISG on a fairness audit toolkit for public-sector AI systems, ensuring that ‘AI for Public Good’ benefits all ethnic and income groups equally.

participants.professional_persona: Wei Jie is a postdoctoral fellow at Nanyang Technological University, researching privacy-enhancing technologies for cross-enterprise data sharing. He frequently participates in joint grant calls with international partners.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to commercialize his privacy-preserving framework as a service for financial and healthcare SMEs in Singapore. He plans to apply for an AI R&D investment grant to scale his prototype.

participants.professional_persona: Sofia is a research lead at an AI startup incubated under the 100 Experiments programme, focusing on AI-driven predictive maintenance for logistics. She previously worked as a lead data scientist at a regional e-commerce company. She is an active participant in Tech x Singapore conferences and industry roundtables.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep knowledge of time-series forecasting, anomaly detection, and reinforcement learning for industrial IoT. Experienced in end-to-end product development from problem scoping to deployment.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to expand her startup's solution to serve the broader ASEAN logistics market. She aims to become an 'Industry Champion' that helps crowd in more researchers into sector-specific AI use cases.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-wjn7hacp
participants.professional_persona: A founder of a small agritech firm that uses AI to help local farms monitor crop health and optimise water usage. He is driven by food security and sustainability concerns.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Hands-on IoT and computer vision integration for agriculture, plus business development experience with Enterprise Singapore’s SME Go Digital programme.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Plans to apply for co-funding under the 100 Experiments programme to develop a low-cost AI pest detection system for smallholder farms in Singapore and the region.

participants.professional_persona: A recent computer science graduate who transitioned into AI through the AI Apprenticeship Programme (AIAP). She currently works on a 100 Experiments project pairing her with public researchers to build a predictive maintenance solution for a logistics SME.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Strong in Python, data wrangling, and basic deep learning. Rapidly gaining hands-on experience in deploying real-world AI solutions for small enterprises.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to specialise in computer vision and eventually start her own AI consultancy serving local SMEs.

participants.professional_persona: A mid-career engineer who switched from semiconductor manufacturing to AI after completing a Company-Led Training Programme under TeSA. He now designs recommendation engines for an e-commerce platform.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Proficient in reinforcement learning, recommendation systems, and cloud deployment. Brings strong systems-thinking from his hardware background.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Hopes to lead the AI practice of a fast-growing tech startup and mentor career-switchers entering the AI field.

participants.professional_persona: A PhD graduate in AI ethics who now works as a responsible AI practitioner at a government-linked technology agency. She ensures that AI systems used in Smart Nation projects are fair, transparent, and accountable.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep knowledge of algorithmic fairness, explainable AI, and AI governance frameworks. Skilled in auditing models for bias and designing compliance checklists.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aspires to be a regional voice in responsible AI, contributing to international standards and advising on AI policy for public good.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-zvlb9ji2
participants.professional_persona: A retired IT professional who now volunteers at a community centre teaching digital literacy to seniors. He sees AI as a tool to help the elderly stay connected and independent.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Broad IT infrastructure knowledge and patient communication skills; recently attended a course on AI basics for seniors organised by IMDA.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to build a simple voice-activated assistant that reminds seniors to take medication and connects them to family, demonstrating that AI for Public Good includes the silver generation.

participants.professional_persona: A product manager turned AI practitioner who led the adoption of Google Cloud's AI toolsets through the AI Trailblazers programme. He now drives AI product strategy for a health-tech startup building a clinical decision support system.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Combines product management with hands-on AI prototyping using APIs and low-code tools. Understands healthcare data privacy and regulatory requirements.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to become a Chief Product Officer in health-tech and help scale AI solutions that improve patient outcomes.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Tan is a mid-career AI researcher who spent 15 years in Silicon Valley before returning to Singapore to join a government-funded AI lab. He specializes in reasoning AI and automated theorem proving. He is a strong advocate for Singapore's AI talent development and volunteers as a mentor in Company-Led Training Programmes.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep expertise in logical reasoning, symbolic AI, and combining neural networks with knowledge graphs. Proficient in leading cross-functional teams to build robust AI systems for complex enterprise needs.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aspires to build a world-class reasoning AI team in Singapore that produces foundational breakthroughs. He also aims to help Singapore companies adopt AI-based decision support tools through the CTO-as-a-Service program.

participants.professional_persona: A self-taught AI developer who honed her skills through hackathons and online courses. She runs a small AI consulting firm that helps retail SMEs implement computer vision for inventory management.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Proficient in edge AI, computer vision, and rapid prototyping. Adept at working with limited budgets and non-technical clients.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to grow her firm into a regional leader in AI for SMEs and actively participates in community bootcamps to upskill other women in tech.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-hss45s3f
participants.professional_persona: Mid-career technologist at a logistics SME who pivoted into AI through TeSA's Company-Led Training Programme. He now leads a small data team and is actively scoping an AI pilot for route optimization under the 100 Experiments programme.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Practical experience in deploying computer vision for warehouse inventory tracking and basic NLP for customer service chatbots. Familiar with AISG's AI Readiness Index and has hands-on knowledge of Google Cloud AI tools from the AI Trailblazers programme.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to become a recognized AI adoption champion in the logistics sector, eventually moving into a CTO-as-a-Service role to help other SMEs adopt AI solutions. Sees AI for Public Good as a way to uplift smaller enterprises through practical, accessible tools.

participants.professional_persona: A former data analyst at a government agency who upskilled via the Career Conversion Programme. He now works as an AI developer at a smart-city solutions company, building models that optimise traffic light timings.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Solid in time-series forecasting, sensor data fusion, and geospatial analytics. Comfortable working with IoT data streams in an urban environment.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to become a technical lead in smart-city AI projects and help Singapore achieve its Smart Nation vision.

participants.professional_persona: Priya is a junior AI researcher at a local startup that won a grant under the AI Trailblazers programme to build generative AI tools for retail. She recently completed her Master's in AI at Singapore Management University, with a thesis on few-shot learning. She is keen to apply her skills to real business problems and gain industry experience.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Solid foundation in few-shot learning, prompt engineering, and large language model fine-tuning. Skilled in building proof-of-concept prototypes quickly for SME partners.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Hopes to grow into a senior AI research role within three years and eventually lead her own research team. She wants to contribute to open-source AI tools tailored for Southeast Asian languages.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-i5f7wz8v
participants.professional_persona: Independent consultant specializing in responsible AI governance, recently engaged by a government agency to help draft sector-specific AI ethics guidelines. She frequently moderates hackathons and ideathons that pair public researchers with industry to solve real-world pain points.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep expertise in AI ethics frameworks, privacy-enhancing technologies, and cross-sector stakeholder engagement. Skilled at facilitating workshops that translate high-level AI for Public Good principles into actionable project criteria.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to establish Singapore as a thought leader in responsible AI deployment, and eventually shape international rules of the road for ethical AI. Sees the focal principle as a lever to ensure AI innovations are robust, safe, and trusted by the public.

participants.professional_persona: Kenji is a visiting researcher from a Japanese university, collaborating with Singapore researchers on resource-efficient AI for manufacturing. He is based at the Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR) for a two-year fellowship. He actively participates in ideathons to identify industry pain points in precision engineering.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expertise in neural architecture search, knowledge distillation, and hardware-aware model design. Familiar with deploying AI on low-power industrial controllers and edge devices.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to establish a joint Japan-Singapore research lab on green AI. He aims to publish foundational papers while also contributing to Singapore's 100 Experiments programme with local manufacturing firms.

participants.professional_persona: Hui Min is a self-taught AI researcher who transitioned from a software engineering role via the Career Conversion Programme. She now works at a fintech startup, developing fraud detection models that comply with responsible AI guidelines. She is a regular speaker at industry workshops on AI adoption for SMEs.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Practical expertise in anomaly detection, graph neural networks for transaction analysis, and model explainability. Strong ability to bridge the gap between research and production deployment in regulated environments.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to become a thought leader in responsible AI for financial services in Singapore. She wants to help shape the government's AI governance framework by providing industry feedback.
